title: ranunculus reptans var. flagellifolius
intro: Ranunculus reptans var. flagellifolius, also known as creeping buttercup, is a perennial plant in the Ranunculaceae family. It is characterized by its white or yellow flowers and creeping habit, often found in moist areas and along streams. The plant is reported to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. Creeping buttercup is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is said to offer health benefits like reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
